steps.title: Find the slope of line k
steps.content:
Starting with $6x + 19y - 7 = 0$, solve for $y$ to find the slope.

$$19y = -6x + 7$$
$$y = -\frac{6}{19}x + \frac{7}{19}$$

The slope of line $k$ is $-\frac{6}{19}$.

steps.title: Apply the perpendicular slope rule
steps.content:
Perpendicular lines have slopes that are negative reciprocals of each other. If the slope of line $k$ is $-\frac{6}{19}$, then the slope of line $j$ is:

$$m_j = -\frac{1}{-\frac{6}{19}} = \frac{19}{6}$$

That matches choice A — A is correct.
